text.title: Notion PM Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Notion PM. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: One Tasks database with Sprint as a relation field. Never create per-sprint databases.
rule.rationale: Per-sprint DBs fragment history + prevent cross-sprint velocity rollups.

rule.statement: Each task is its own DB page, not a sub-bullet inside a sprint page. Only DB pages are API-queryable.
rule.rationale: Sub-bullets aren't filterable / sortable / updatable by agents.

rule.statement: ≤20 properties per database. Archive unused properties instead of accumulating.
rule.rationale: Notion DB performance drops with many properties + complex formulas; ≤20 keeps the workspace responsive.

rule.statement: Status field uses native 'status' property type (not 'select'). Required for kanban grouping + board view.
rule.rationale: Select-typed status loses native not-started/in-progress/complete grouping.

rule.statement: API code paginates with start_cursor (max 100 items / request); adds 350ms delay between writes during bulk ops.
rule.rationale: Notion API caps at 3 req/sec; pagination is mandatory because page_size never returns >100.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-notion-pm.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
